description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane featuring a streamlined fuselage with a distinctive Singapore Airlines livery, including a dark blue tail fin adorned with a gold logo and a horizontal gold stripe along the body. The aircraft has two large turbofan engines mounted under its low, swept-back wings, which are equipped with upward-curved winglets at the tips. The windows are arranged in a single row along the length of the fuselage, and the cockpit is positioned at the very front, with a sharply pointed nose. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a classic tail design. The landing gear is not visible in these images, suggesting it is likely retractable and currently stowed. The overall appearance is sleek and modern, optimized for long-distance commercial air travel.
description: A modern Singapore Airlines commercial airplane with swept low wings, underwing turbofan engines, and a blue-and-gold livery. The aircraft features a pointed nose, single row of windows, and a conventional tail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the Singapore Airlines livery, single row of windows, and both engines under the wings.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, winglets, and the streamlined fuselage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the tail logo and engine placement.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the pointed nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetrical placement of the engines and wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with long, swept-back wings featuring upward-curved winglets and a slight dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible, inferred from design)
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multi-seat configuration and multiple front-facing windows
